name: Eddie Shu
description: American swing and jazz multi-instrumentalist with high proficiency on tenor and alto saxophone, clarinet, trumpet, harmonica, and accordion
abstract: Eddie Shu (ne Edward Shulman; 18 August 1918 New York City – 4 July 1986) was an American jazz musician who played saxophone, clarinet, trumpet, harmonica, and accordion. He was also a comedic ventriloquist.

concerts.label: All-Star Jazz Concert and Recording Session
Midnight Performance: Jazz at the Philharmonic
Midnight Performance: Jazz at the Philharmonic

about_quotes.speaker: Big Chielf Moore
about_quotes.evidence: The transcript mentions Eddie Shu taking Joe Darensbourg's place in Louie Bellson's band, but the interviewee (Big Chief Moore) does not speak about a personal relationship with Eddie Shu.

about_quotes.speaker: Patricia Willard
about_quotes.evidence: I went in there and I worked I guess about a week, with Eddie Shu ... so I met him.
